decisions.decision: Per-zero claim is FALSE — 3-4% of zeros increase r when removed
decisions.rationale: Direct computation at 6 heights, growing violation fraction

decisions.decision: Mean influence is robustly negative at all heights
decisions.rationale: mean(delta_r) = -0.018 to -0.025 across T=300 to T=24000

decisions.decision: Deficit ratio grows as 0.06*log(T), extrapolated failure T~3e8
decisions.rationale: Shape factor c=1.55 constant, growth from shrinking gaps

decisions.decision: Paper revised to v3: numerical observation, not theorem
decisions.rationale: Honest about gaps per Grok feedback and our own analysis

next_action: Close gap 1 (prove E[delta_r] < 0) via moment bounds on |zeta'| at zeros, or compute mean influence at T~10^11 from Odlyzko data to extend verification
context_notes: Session 11 was a deep diagnostic of the elimination approach. Three key discoveries: (1) the per-zero exclusion claim is false for 3-4% outlier zeros, (2) the deficit ratio grows as log(T), (3) r(T) drops to 0.63 at T=2.68e11 but stays well above Conrey's threshold. The paper has been revised from 'proof of 93.6%' to 'numerical density bound verified across 11 orders of magnitude'. Three gaps to a theorem identified: mean influence, r lower bound, additivity.
